hero.typing: I build, configure and secure networks.

about.title: About
about.objectiveText: Become a network & system technician or administrator
about.objectiveDesc: Passionate about IT infrastructure and network monitoring
about.aboutText1: Passionate about computer networking, I am particularly interested in designing and configuring complex network infrastructures (MPLS, OSPF, EIGRP), as well as monitoring and maintaining IT infrastructures.
about.aboutText2: I am also developing system administration skills through projects and hands-on practice. I am actively seeking opportunities to deepen my network and system administration skills in a stimulating technical environment.

reflection.title: My Reflection
reflection.portfolioTitle: What my portfolio says about me
reflection.portfolioText1: My portfolio reflects my <1>passion for network infrastructure</1> and my commitment to mastering the entire technical chain, from design to monitoring.
reflection.portfolioText2: It shows that I am <1>methodical</1>, <2>curious</2>, and capable of leading technical projects from start to finish. I take the time to document my achievements and explain my reasoning.
reflection.portfolioText3: As a future Networking & Telecommunications professional, I strive to develop a holistic vision: architecture, security, automation, and monitoring form a coherent whole in my projects.
but1Items: First steps with Packet Tracer and GNS3
Setting up DHCP, DNS and HTTP servers (Apache/Nginx).
but2Items: First scripts to automate simple system tasks.
reflection.improvement1Text: Deepen Cloud infrastructure management (AWS, Azure) and Edge services to optimize multimedia content delivery and application latency.
reflection.improvement2Text: Develop my Python skills for Network Automation and deepen Ansible with complex roles and CI/CD.

projects.title: Technical Projects
projects.docUnavailableDesc: The report is not yet available for this project.
projects.noPdf: No PDF report has been attached to this project yet.

list.title: Network Monitoring with Cacti & SNMP
list.description: Deployment of a monitoring architecture on a segmented virtualized network (LAN/NAT).
list.contexte: As part of an academic project (SAE), the goal was to simulate a partitioned corporate network for surveillance purposes, without direct client access to the Internet.
list.objectif: Implement a complete monitoring chain (Manager/Agent) via SNMP protocol to monitor the availability and resources of a heterogeneous fleet.
realisation: Design of a virtualized topology (3 VMs): Creation of an isolated LAN behind an Ubuntu gateway configured with NAT (iptables, ip_forward)
Installation and configuration of the LAMP stack (Linux, Apache, MariaDB, PHP) required for Cacti operation
Deployment of SNMP agents (snmpd) on Debian client machines and community configuration
Integration of hosts into the Cacti console and creation of performance graphs (Interface traffic, CPU load, Ping)
Outage tests: Validation of incident detection (Hosts transitioning to 'Down' state in the console)
list.resultats: Functional infrastructure enabling real-time visualization of fleet health (UP/DOWN) and resource usage history through RRDtool graphs.

list.title: Multi-Site Monitoring TIG (Telegraf-InfluxDB-Grafana)
list.description: Real-time monitoring of a distributed server fleet (Paris/NY/London) with mapping and alerting.
list.contexte: Need to monitor in real-time the system health (CPU) and network quality (Latency) of Linux servers distributed geographically (Paris, New York, London) on a single interface.
list.objectif: Design and deploy a complete containerized TIG monitoring stack (Telegraf, InfluxDB, Grafana) to visualize metrics and asset geolocation.
realisation: Distributed Architecture: Master Server (Docker/InfluxDB/Grafana) and Telegraf Agents (Nodes) on 3 Debian 12 VMs
Advanced Visualization: Creation of a 'Weather' Dashboard with Worldmap (GPS tag injection) and CPU Gauges (visual alerting > 80%)
Network Metrology: Monitoring connectivity and inter-site latency via active Ping probes
System Troubleshooting: Bypassing GPG restrictions on Debian 12 (Bookworm) and fixing execution permissions for the Ping plugin
Data Engineering: Using Grafana Transformations to convert GPS coordinates (String) to numbers on the fly
list.resultats: Operational monitoring infrastructure providing an instant geographic view. Validation of skills across the complete data chain (Collection > Storage > Visualization) and troubleshooting in containerized environments.

list.title: End-to-End IoT & Monitoring Chain
list.description: Temperature/Humidity data acquisition (MQTT, InfluxDB) and threshold alert setup (Grafana).
list.contexte: The goal was to design an autonomous environmental telemetry system capable of not only collecting data (Temperature/Humidity) but also being remotely controlled.
list.objectif: Implement a complete IoT architecture (Edge to Cloud) using standard protocols (MQTT) and a containerized visualization stack.
realisation: Microcontroller programming on Pycom (MicroPython): Reading Pysense sensors and managing WiFi/MQTT connectivity
Deployment of a Docker infrastructure on Debian 12 hosting: InfluxDB (Database), Grafana (Visualization) and Mosquitto (Broker)
Development of a Python 'Bridge' script to intercept MQTT topics and inject metrics into InfluxDB
Setup of a Node-RED control Dashboard allowing real-time commands (Pause/Resume) to sensors
Test recipes: Configuration of alert thresholds (Alerting) to detect critical temperature exceedances
list.resultats: Operational system enabling real-time visualization of environmental curves and automatic alert triggering.

list.title: Server Fleet Automation (Ansible)
list.description: Automated management of a Linux server fleet: centralized configuration, installation and updates.
list.contexte: Managing servers one by one is repetitive and error-prone. I needed a method to administer multiple machines simultaneously without connecting to each one individually.
list.objectif: Set up a 'control center' (Management Node) capable of sending configurations and updates remotely to an entire fleet of machines, automatically and securely.
realisation: Creation of a virtual lab with 3 Debian servers connected on a private network to simulate a company environment.
Setup of a secure 'remote control': SSH key configuration to allow the master server to manage others without passwords.
Creation of simple scripts (Playbooks) to automate common tasks: installing software (Nmap, NTP), running system updates (apt update) and copying files.
Using inventory to precisely target which machines should receive which configuration.
list.resultats: I can now configure or update all my servers by running a single command. What used to take 1 hour manually is now done in 2 minutes, with no risk of oversight.

list.title: Linux Router & Secure Architecture (DMZ/VLAN)
list.description: Turning a Debian server into a router/firewall: Segmentation, NAT/PAT and 802.1Q VLANs.
list.contexte: In environments where dedicated equipment (Cisco/Fortinet) is unavailable, it is essential to know how to deploy advanced network functions directly via the Linux operating system.
list.objectif: Design a robust software security gateway ensuring routing, filtering and segmentation between a private LAN, a public DMZ and Internet access.
realisation: Configuration of routing (IP Forwarding) and static addressing on Debian 12
Setup of perimeter security: iptables rules for PAT (Outbound) and DNAT (Web port redirection to DMZ)
Logical segmentation: Creation of virtual interfaces for VLAN trunking (802.1Q) to clients
Service deployment: Apache Web Server in isolated zone (DMZ) accessible from outside
Technical validation: Frame analysis with Tcpdump to verify VLAN packet tagging
list.resultats: Functional and secure infrastructure. Validation of zone isolation (LAN vs DMZ) and web service accessibility via port redirection.

list.title: MPLS Architecture & Virtualization
list.description: Design of a complete L3VPN service provider network: Client isolation (VRF), Routing (OSPF/BGP) and Protocol analysis.
list.contexte: Simulation of an ISP backbone network that must interconnect the remote sites of a client ('Carrefour') while ensuring complete traffic isolation from other clients.
list.objectif: Implement a complete MPLS L3VPN architecture: Internal routing (OSPF), Label switching (LDP), Isolation (VRF) and VPNv4 route propagation (MP-BGP).
realisation: Configuration of the Backbone IGP (OSPF Area 0) and activation of LDP protocol on core interfaces
Creation of virtual routing instances (VRF) on PE routers to isolate the 'Carrefour' client
Configuration of MP-BGP sessions for VPNv4 route exchange between border routers (PE)
Setup of bidirectional redistribution between client routing (OSPF) and operator BGP
Wireshark Analysis: Dissection of the MPLS label stack (Transport Label vs VPN Label) and BGP Update messages
list.resultats: Operational client site interconnection (Ping validated). Mastery of MPLS forwarding mechanisms (Swap/Pop) and BGP VPNv4 signaling.

list.title: High Availability SQL Cluster (MariaDB)
list.description: Implementation of a distributed database architecture with bidirectional replication and security.
list.contexte: To ensure service continuity of a critical application (e-commerce type), an isolated database represents a single point of failure (SPOF). Data redundancy was necessary.
list.objectif: Design a complex relational database (Stored procedures, Triggers) and deploy Master-Slave replication (then bidirectional) to ensure high availability.
realisation: Modeling and creation of the 'Amazon' database: Relational tables (Foreign keys), test data insertion
SQL Automation: Development of stored procedures (simplified client addition) and Triggers (critical stock alert) for data integrity
Security and RBAC: Creation of least-privilege users (Manager vs Consultant) and network access restriction
Replication setup: Configuration of 'my.cnf' files (server-id, log-bin), replication user management and initial synchronization (SQL Dump)
Troubleshooting: Resolving inter-node connectivity issues (UFW Firewall configuration port 3306) and replication thread diagnostics (Slave_IO/SQL_Running)
list.resultats: Operational database cluster. Bidirectional replication ensures that any write on the Master is instantly propagated to the Slave, guaranteeing data resilience.

list.title: Web Solution & Automation: Si'La Fée Zen
list.description: E-wellness booking platform with client authentication, relational database and CRM automation.
list.contexte: A wellness practitioner wanted to professionalize her business: moving from manual appointment scheduling to a digital ecosystem managing her clients and schedule.
list.objectif: Create an automated digital ecosystem: online booking, member verification by phone and calendar synchronization without human intervention.
realisation: Deployment and Hosting: Going live via Netlify with CI/CD pipeline (GitHub) for high availability and automatic updates.
Data Architecture (Supabase): Design of a relational SQL schema to manage bookings and the 'Private Club' member registry.
Identity Verification: Phone number authentication to validate club access and limit spam.
CRM Automation (Make): Workflow creation for instant transactional emails and automatic calendar sync when appointments are booked.
Admin Dashboard (Back-Office): Development of a private management interface allowing the client to moderate club memberships and manage her schedule in real-time.
User Experience (UX): Responsive React interface allowing bookings from a smartphone in under 3 clicks.
list.resultats: A turnkey solution allowing the client to focus solely on her massages, with the site handling all administrative and logistical tasks.

list.title: Active Directory Administration (Windows Server 2016)
list.description: Deployment of a domain infrastructure: Domain Controller, User management and Workstation joining.
list.contexte: To centralize IT fleet administration (simulated for a university institute), it was necessary to transition from Workgroup management (peer-to-peer) to a centralized Domain architecture.
list.objectif: Install and configure a Domain Controller (DC) on Windows Server 2016, structure the directory and validate client authentication.
realisation: Installation of Windows Server 2016 OS and network configuration (static IP, local DNS)
Server promotion to Domain Controller and creation of the 'rtdomtest.com' forest
Directory architecture: Creation of Organizational Units (OU) to reflect the institute's structure
Identity Management (IAM): Creation of users and security groups (Management, Teachers, Students)
Client Integration: DNS configuration of workstations and domain joining to enable unified logon
list.resultats: Operational domain with a structured user base. Client workstations can authenticate on the network via the Domain Controller.

experience.title: Experience
tasks: HDD to SSD disk cloning for optimization
Installation and configuration of business software
Preventive and corrective maintenance of IT equipment
